plain: A name that is stored INSIDE persistent state — in users' browsers (cookie name, localStorage key), in R2 object keys, in hashes. Renaming it orphans the state.
why: The whole rename boundary hinges on telling these apart from display-only names.

plain: Deliberately keep an old name forever on an invisible surface (R2 bucket, data dirname) because renaming it carries risk and zero visible payoff.
why: Cheapest correct answer for most state-carrying ids.

plain: During a rename transition the code reads the NEW name first and falls back to the OLD (env var, cookie, localStorage key), so nothing breaks the day the switch flips.
why: The only safe way to rename an id that lives in other people's browsers/shells.

plain: The name field in plugin.json — it derives the /trainlint:* command prefix, the cache path, and the trainlint@trainlint install key in settings.json.
why: Renaming it is an uninstall/reinstall event, not a string swap.

plain: Your server-side identity is sha256 of the .token FILE CONTENT — the file's path/dirname is irrelevant.
why: Proves data_root can move without losing the paired identity.
